message:
feat(cli): add file watching mode, plugins command, and hooks directory support

Extend CLI capabilities with file change watching, plugin management,
directory-based hooks loading, and improved spawn hook handling.

File Watch Mode (internal/cli/watch.go):

New flags for file-triggered commands:
- --pattern flag specifies glob pattern to watch (e.g., "*.go")
- --command flag specifies command to send to agents on change
- Uses internal/watcher package for fsnotify integration

runFileWatch() implementation:
- Validates --command is provided with --pattern
- Creates watcher with recursive directory monitoring
- Handler filters events matching --pattern glob
- Triggers command on matching panes via tmux.SendKeys()
- Respects agent type filters (--cc, --cod, --gmi)

Example: ntm watch myproject --pattern="*.go" --command="go test ./..."

Plugins Command (internal/cli/plugins.go):

New ntm plugins subcommand:
- ntm plugins list shows installed agent and command plugins
- Loads from ~/.config/ntm/agents/ and ~/.config/ntm/commands/
- Tabular output with NAME, ALIAS, DESCRIPTION columns
- Handles empty plugin state gracefully

Registered in root.go command tree.

Hooks Directory Loading (internal/hooks/config.go):

Multi-source hook loading:
- LoadHooksFromDirectory() scans hooks/*.toml files
- DefaultCommandHooksDir() returns ~/.config/ntm/hooks/
- LoadAllCommandHooks() combines: hooks.toml + hooks/*.toml + config.toml
- Enables modular hook organization

New hook events:
- EventPreKill and EventPostKill for kill command lifecycle
- Added to AllCommandEvents() registry

Spawn Hook Safety (internal/cli/spawn.go):

--no-hooks flag:
- Disables hook execution when set
- Useful for debugging or fast spawns
- hookExec nil-check safety throughout execution

Nil executor handling:
- Check hookExec != nil before HasHooksForEvent() calls
- Prevents nil pointer dereference when hooks disabled

Config Updates (internal/config/config.go):

New check_project_inbox prompt:
- Category: Coordination
- Instructs agents to check mail inbox
- References 'ntm mail inbox' command

Test Coverage:

internal/tmux/session_test.go:
- TestParseAgentFromTitle for agent type/variant/tags parsing
- Covers cc, cod, gmi prefixes with variants and tag brackets

tests/e2e/robot_mode_test.go:
- TestRobotStatusWithLiveSession with real tmux session
- Spawns session with claude+codex agents
- Verifies robot-status JSON includes session and agent count
- Proper cleanup via t.Cleanup()

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+package cli
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"text/tabwriter"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/config"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/plugins"
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+)
+
+func newPluginsCmd() *cobra.Command {
+	cmd := &cobra.Command{
+		Use:   "plugins",
+		Short: "Manage and list installed plugins",
+	}
+
+	cmd.AddCommand(newPluginsListCmd())
+	return cmd
+}
+
+func newPluginsListCmd() *cobra.Command {
+	return &cobra.Command{
+		Use:   "list",
+		Short: "List installed agent and command plugins",
+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+			configDir := filepath.Dir(config.DefaultPath())
+			
+			// Load Agent Plugins
+		agentsDir := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents")
+		agentPlugins, _ := plugins.LoadAgentPlugins(agentsDir)
+
+			// Load Command Plugins
+			cmdDir := filepath.Join(configDir, "commands")
+			commandPlugins, _ := plugins.LoadCommandPlugins(cmdDir)
+
+			if len(agentPlugins) == 0 && len(commandPlugins) == 0 {
+				fmt.Println("No plugins installed.")
+				return nil
+			}
+
+			w := tabwriter.NewWriter(os.Stdout, 0, 0, 3, ' ', 0)
+			
+			if len(agentPlugins) > 0 {
+				fmt.Println("Agent Plugins:")
+				fmt.Fprintln(w, "NAME\tALIAS\tDESCRIPTION")
+				for _, p := range agentPlugins {
+					alias := p.Alias
+					if alias == "" {
+						alias = "-"
+					}
+					fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\t%s\t%s\n", p.Name, alias, p.Description)
+				}
+				w.Flush()
+				fmt.Println()
+			}
+
+			if len(commandPlugins) > 0 {
+				fmt.Println("Command Plugins:")
+				fmt.Fprintln(w, "NAME\tUSAGE\tDESCRIPTION")
+				for _, p := range commandPlugins {
+					fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\t%s\t%s\n", p.Name, p.Usage, p.Description)
+				}
+				w.Flush()
+				fmt.Println()
+			}
+
+			return nil
+		},
+	}
+}
